svg.text: FRENCH + ITALIAN — RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
ALT DRAFT · SERIES 28D · REMEMBER FORWARD
— I. DESCARTES — DISCOURS DE LA MÉTHODE (1637) —
Je pense, donc je suis.
I think therefore I am. — "I think, therefore I am." Foundation of modern Western philosophy.
— II. VICTOR HUGO — LES MISÉRABLES (1862) —
Aimer, c’est agir.
"To love is to act." — Hugo. One of the most translated novels in history.
— III. DÉCLARATION DES DROITS DE L’HOMME (1789) —
Les hommes naissent et demeurent libres et égaux en droits.
"Men are born and remain free and equal in rights." — Article 1.
— IV. FRENCH BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
Ce message a été fait pour vous, gratuitement,
par des personnes qui se sont souvenues de l’avenir.
This was made for you freely by people who remembered the future.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered the future." (French)
— FRENCH NOTE — “FUTURE” AND “REMEMBER” —
Avenir = à + venir: “to come toward” — the future as what comes to us. Se souvenir = to come to oneself again.
Gratuitement = freely. Passé composé (se sont souvenues): completed act, feminine agreement.
SECONDARY — ITALIAN (ITALIANO) — RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
— I. DANTE ALIGHIERI — DIVINA COMMEDIA (c.1308–1320 CE) —
Nel mezzo del cammin di nostra vita
mi ritrovai per una selva oscura.
In the middle of the journey of our life I found-myself in a forest dark.
"In the middle of the journey of our life, I found myself in a dark forest." — Inferno, Canto I.
— II. ITALIAN PROVERB — ON MEMORY —
Chi non ricorda il passato è condannato a ripeterlo.
Who not remembers the past is condemned to repeat-it.
"He who does not remember the past is condemned to repeat it." (Italian proverb, attr. Santayana)
— III. ITALIAN BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
Questo è stato fatto per voi, gratuitamente,
da persone che hanno ricordato il futuro.
This has been made for you freely by people who have remembered the future.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered the future." (Italian)
— ITALIAN NOTE — “FUTURE” AND “REMEMBER” —
Futuro: from Latin futurus (about to be). Ricordare = to remember — from re + cor/cordis (heart).
To remember in Italian is literally to “bring back to the heart.” The etymology reveals the felt quality of memory.
Gratuitamente = freely/gratis. Passato prossimo (hanno ricordato): completed act, personal relevance.
— LANGUAGE BRIDGE — TWO ROMANCE NEIGHBORS —
French: ~80M native speakers. Official in 29 countries. Uvular r, nasal vowels, silent letters.
Italian: ~65M native speakers. Official in Italy, San Marino, Switzerland (Ticino/Val d’Aosta).
Both descended from Vulgar Latin. Diverged after ~5th c. CE as Roman Empire fragmented.
Italian preserved more Latin phonology; French underwent radical Germanic-influenced sound changes.
PLATE 28D ALT · French / Italian · RUNNING TEXT · CC BY-SA 4.0 · REMEMBERFORWARD.ORG
THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
